_README: Per-vehicle briefing overrides for generate_pdf_report.py (kept in the skill, not vehicles.json, for self-containment). ARTIC GVM TIERS — domain knowledge for the load points: an articulated HGV's gross vehicle mass falls into tiers, lightest to heaviest — (1) TRACTOR-ONLY / bobtail ~10 t = the lightest cluster, NOT a real operating mass -> EXCLUDE it (set 'unladen_band_t' lo above it); (2) TRACTOR + EMPTY TRAILER ~16-19 t (rig-dependent) = UNLADEN; (3) LOADED operations = higher, operation-dependent, up to the rated GVW (~42 t) = LADEN. Set the band by AI JUDGEMENT of the observed GVM histogram/KDE, NOT by the default KDE split (which merges the unladen shoulder into the dominant laden peak and so mislabels a mostly-laden vehicle's unladen) nor by the legacy tertile (which lets the bobtail contaminate the lightest third). For such artics set 'unladen_band_t': [lo, hi] (and optionally 'laden_min_t'): unladen = data points in [lo, hi) (tractor + empty trailer), laden = GVM >= laden_min (default hi), bobtail (< lo) excluded. Omit to fall back to KDE light/heavy clustering. 'unladen_mass_t' can pin the unladen marker mass explicitly (use when the unladen cluster is sparse). 'note' records the judgement + provenance. Band takes priority over SINGLE_GROUP_REGS / LEGACY_TERTILE_REGS / KDE in _compute_load_points.

EX74JXW.note: Scania 23P, ARTIC, rated GVW 42 t (518 kWh). All-data GVM histogram (n=240, KDE peaks 12.7 / 24.6 t): bobtail tractor-only ~12.7 t (10-13 t, 16 pts) EXCLUDED; UNLADEN tractor + empty trailer = the sparse 13-19 t shoulder (~10 pts) — marker PINNED to 17 t (sparse cluster, canonical tractor+trailer figure); LADEN = loaded GVM >= 19 t (bulk 19-37 t, median ~25 t); full = 42 t rated GVW (observed tops ~37 t bar one 43 t outlier -> dashed projection). Tightened from the earlier window-data band [14,20] because on all-data that pulled unladen up into the 19-20 t laden start.

CMZ6260.note: Volvo FH Electric, ARTIC, rated GVW ~42 t. AI judgement of the all-data GVM histogram (n=327, KDE peaks 10.5 / 19.6 / 23.8 t): three tiers — bobtail tractor-only ~10.5 t (the 10-12 t bin, 12 pts) EXCLUDED; UNLADEN tractor + empty trailer = the 14-18 t band (~27 pts, data median ~16.4 t) — marker PINNED to 17 t (canonical tractor+trailer; data supports ~16-17 t); LADEN = loaded operations GVM >= 18 t (dominant runs 18-26 t, median ~22 t); full = 42 t rated GVW (observed tops ~26 t bar one 46 t outlier -> dashed projection). The DEFAULT KDE split mislabels this mostly-laden vehicle — merges the ~17 t unladen shoulder into the dominant ~19.6 t laden peak (wrong unladen ~19 t); the band corrects unladen to ~17 t.

YK73WFN.note: Volvo FH Electric, ARTIC, rated GVW 42 t. All-data GVM histogram (n=838, KDE peaks 11.1 / 19.4 / 27.8 / 39.2 t): bobtail tractor-only ~11 t (10-12 t, **106 pts**) EXCLUDED — this is exactly why the legacy tertile mislabels YK73 (the 106 bobtail points contaminate the lightest third); UNLADEN tractor + empty trailer ~19 t = the dominant 18-22 t cluster (data-driven; this rig's empty is ~19 t, heavier than CMZ6260's ~17 t); LADEN = loaded operations GVM >= 24 t (clusters ~28 t and ~40 t, median ~34-36 t); full = 42 t rated GVW (observed reaches ~42 t). Switched from LEGACY_TERTILE to an AI-judged band (band has priority) so the bobtail is excluded; this changes the earlier Nestle-approved tertile baseline (1.16/1.79) — done at user request (all-data + new workflow).

YN25RSY.note: Mercedes-Benz eActros 600, ARTIC, ~600 kWh, rated GVW ~42 t. All-data GVM histogram (n=35, KDE peaks 11.6 / 19.6 / 23.9 t): bobtail ~11.6 t (1 pt) EXCLUDED; UNLADEN tractor + empty trailer ~19 t = the 17-21 t cluster (~14 pts; eActros empty is ~19 t, heavier due to the large battery); LADEN = loaded operations GVM >= 21 t (21-29 t, median ~24 t); full = 42 t rated GVW (observed tops ~27 t -> dashed projection). Switched from SINGLE_GROUP (laden-only, no unladen point) to an AI-judged band (band has priority) because the data shows a clear ~19 t unladen cluster distinct from the ~24 t laden.
